channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP lawmakers unleash proposal to reverse Biden's 'ill-informed' policies crippling crucial industry
item.description: Republican lawmakers are introducing legislation to undo a method used by the Biden administration to limit the ability to produce oil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Institute of Peace headquarters transfer is a done deal even as lawsuit continues to unfold
item.description: There is no answer yet on the lawsuit filed by Institute of Peace board members who were fired amid a remaking of the independent think tank.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration halts dozens of research grants at Princeton University
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has halted dozens of research grants at Princeton University, the latest Ivy League school to see its federal money threatened in a pressure campaign targeting the nation’s top universities.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Accused MS-13 leader nabbed by Patel's FBI to remain in custody for now, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge in Virginia ruled Tuesday there was enough evidence to keep accused MS-13 top leader Henrry Josue Villatoro Santos in custody after his arrest in late March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Results: Wisconsin Supreme Court election
item.description: Tuesday's outcome in Wisconsin could have major implications for abortion rights and congressional district maps.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Results: Florida District 1 special election
item.description: Valimont raised almost $6.5 million compared to Patronis' $2.1 million, according to Federal Elections Commission filings.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Results: Florida District 6 special election
item.description: During November's presidential election, President Donald Trump won the district on Florida’s eastern coast by 30 points.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's changes to a $42 billion broadband program could be a win for Musk's Starlink
item.description: After years of planning, the Trump administration is overhauling a federal universal broadband initiative to open the door to Musk's Starlink satellite service.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump's transgender military ban dealt legal blow after appeals court ruling
item.description: A federal appeals court denied the Trump administration’s request to enforce its transgender military ban, keeping a lower court’s injunction in place for now.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans face possible rebellion over hardball tactic on proxy voting
item.description: An effort by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) to stop Rep. Anna Paulina Luna’s (R-Fla.) push for allowing proxy voting for new parents is on thin ice as a number of Republicans warn they may vote against leadership’s hardball tactic.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin voters to choose top education official and consider voter ID amendment
item.description: Wisconsin is electing its top education official, who will guide policies affecting K-12 schools during President Donald Trump’s second term. Tuesday’s vote pits the teachers union-backed incumbent Jill Underly against Republican-supported critic Brittany Kinser.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Election Day: Suburban voters head to the polls
item.description: Several elections will take place in Chicago-area suburbs on Tuesday, including a notable one in Dolton where incumbent Tiffany Henyard will run against Jason House, who won the primary election in February.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: Hundreds protest the ICE detainment of a U of M graduate student
item.description: Protesters gathered at the University of Minnesota Twin Cities campus after an international graduate student was detained by immigration officials on Thursday. During the rally, it was announced that a student at Minnesota State University, Mankato was also detained.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Matt Sepic, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: More Republicans want the US to focus on ceasefires in Ukraine and Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — While most Americans disapprove of President Donald Trump’s handling of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, the conflict is not weighing as heavily on his public perception as it did on President Joe Biden, a new poll shows.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada is testing Republican support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With President Donald Trump's so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Entire staff at federal agency that funds libraries and museums put on leave
item.description: The staff of the Institute of Museum and Library Services was placed on administrative leave Monday morning, following a meeting between IMLS leadership and DOGE staff.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong, MPR News

item.title: Luna accuses House Freedom Caucus of smear; exits group over parental proxy voting battle
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) announced Monday that she is leaving the House Freedom Caucus amid her battle with GOP leadership and hard-line Republicans over her push to allow proxy voting for new parents.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal employees union sues over Trump move to block collective bargaining
item.description: A federal employee union has sued to block a Trump administration order that would strip bargaining rights at more than a dozen departments.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Session crossroads: Deadlines loom, budget decisions near, sports stadium requests simmer
item.description: Legislators will mull over requests for sports facility upgrades, as they get a clearer idea for the budget they’ll be working with.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams' lawyer urges ‘prompt resolution’ ahead of election deadline
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – With a filing deadline approaching in New York City’s upcoming mayoral election, Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer has urged a judge to "promptly" issue a decision in the mayor’s bribery case. Petitions are due this week for candidates to appear on a party's primary ballot.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: From Seattle to Miami, anti-Musk protesters gather at hundreds of Tesla locations
item.description: At a rally in Los Angeles, 65-year-old Phil Ansell said he wanted to participate because "I want to do everything possible to protect democracy in this country.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters. The request comes hours before President Donald Trump’s ally plans the giveaway at an evening rally in Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota food shelves worried as USDA cancels truckloads of food
item.description: This week, the U.S. Department of Agriculture canceled almost half of food shelf orders from Minnesota farmers under the federal Commodity Credit Corporation. Organizations providing nutrition programs say they are concerned about how to provide for their clientele.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: The Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party will elect a new chair on Saturday
item.description: Two Democrats are vying to lead the state’s DFL Party. Members of the DFL will vote Saturday for a replacement to longtime party chair Ken Martin who left earlier this year to head up the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
